ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Collects and synthesizes all information relevant to purchasing decisions; Identifies and recruits potential suppliers of the resources necessary for Researches production
Negotiates price with suppliers and purchases desired resources Sources
ActivityCycle.Examples: Buy-side Broker, Auction Houses, Employee Recruitment

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Documents the proof of purchase and/or ownership Invoices
Plans and initiates the transportation of purchased resources Routes
ActivityCycle.IO: C2ii, C2iii, C3ii, C3iii, C4ii, C4iii

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Sends compensation and retrieves the purchased resources Transports
Documents the transportation and arrival of acquired resources; if necessary, temporarily stores and reroutes resources
ActivityCycle.Colloquial: Documents the arrival of the resource and reroutes if necessary

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Receives, unloads, secures, and, when necessary, later re-initiates transfer of the relevant resources
Stores and Manages Resources Stores
ActivityCycle.Examples: AP: Storage Facilities, Waste Disposal RP: Library

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Documents Inventory Catalogs
Researches and aggregates specifications for product design Researches
ActivityCycle.Examples: General Research, Land Surveying, Laboratories, Space Programs, Detectives

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Synthesizes research to create the product design Designs
ActivityCycle.Examples: Architecture Firms, Workforce Training, Graphic Design

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Validates product designs Tests
Plans the production process Plans Production
Produces the product Makes
ActivityCycle.Examples: Manufacturing, Software Developers, Food Producers, Construction

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Performs routine maintenance and fixes on the product Maintains
Diagnoses defective products Diagnoses
Performs non-routine repairs on the product Repairs
Aggregates and manages information relating to the product and the production process Documents
ActivityCycle.Colloquial: Checks that the product is ready for sale
ActivityCycle.Examples: Product Lifecycle Management, Health Record Management

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Researches, identifies, and recruits potential customers Advertises
Negotiates the price and sells the product Sells
ActivityCycle.Examples: RP: Retail and Online Stores, Dealerships, Vending Machines AP: Sell-side Brokerage

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Documents the sale and invoices the client Bills
Plans and initiates the retrieval of compensation and the transportation of the sold product Processes
Sends the product and retrieves the compensation Exchanges
ActivityCycle.Examples: Payment Networks, Money Transfers, Bill Pay Services

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Documents the transport and receipt of compensation; if necessary, temporarily stores and reroutes compensation
ActivityCycle.Colloquial: Records that payment has been received

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Receives and secures assets Secures
ActivityCycle.Colloquial: Loads and unloads money from accounts
ActivityCycle.Examples: Fraud Management, Bank Security, Identity Protection

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Stores money, provides funds, and makes external investments Invests
ActivityCycle.Examples: AP: Banking RP: Insurance Companies, Car Rental Agency

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Aggregates, stores, and manages information relevant to sales and financial assets Accounts
ActivityCycle.Colloquial: Takes inventory of money and assets

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Collects and synthesizes information relevant to strategy decisions Researches
ActivityCycle.Examples: Risk Assessment, SWOT Analysis, Actuarial Services

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Plans system's objectives, products, and plan Strategizes
ActivityCycle.Examples: Investment Advisory, Social Worker, Management Consulting

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Signs off on the strategy and ensures that the objectives are in line with the entity's overall goals Validates
Plans the implementation of the strategic plan by allocating resources and planning the process
Implements plan; directs and communicates with workforce Directs
Supervises work and compares progress to plans Monitors
ActivityCycle.Examples: Regulation, Supervisors, Legal Services, Compliance Officers

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Records performance of the system, accounts for assets, and evaluates resources Measures
ActivityCycle.Colloquial: Collects data about the system's operations

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Analyzes data gathered in 4.3.1 and uses it to draw conclusions and make predictions Analyzes
ActivityCycle.Colloquial: Analyzes data and creates a management report

ActivityCycle.FormalDefinition: Signs off on reports generated in 4.3.2 and compares against plan to see if objectives were achieved. Validates

Resources.Examples: Outer Space, Undeveloped Land, Air, or Water
Property, Claimed Land, Air, or Water
